articles.title: LBE Pipeline Hits 16.5 Million Square Feet, JLL Reports
articles.summary: JLL's new report reveals a massive 16.5 million square-foot development pipeline for location-based entertainment, signaling continued investor and developer confidence in the sector. This level of expansion underscores the growing demand for FEC-style attractions as anchor tenants in retail and mixed-use developments. FEC operators and investors should monitor this pipeline for competitive pressures and real estate opportunities.

articles.title: Periscope Equity Invests in Amusement Connect Specialty Payments Platform
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope Equity has made a strategic investment in Amusement Connect, a payments platform built specifically for the amusement industry. The deal signals growing institutional interest in fintech solutions tailored to FEC operations. For operators, this investment could accelerate product development and broader adoption of specialized payment tools across the sector.

articles.title: ROLLER Partners with Adyen to Offer Embedded Financing for 3,000+ Venues
articles.summary: ROLLER, a leading venue management platform used by FECs and attractions worldwide, has partnered with Adyen to provide fast, embedded financing through a new Capital product available to over 3,000 venues. This gives FEC operators access to working capital directly through their existing ROLLER platform without lengthy traditional loan processes. The partnership could be a game-changer for smaller operators needing flexible funding for equipment, renovations, or seasonal cash flow.

articles.title: $3 Billion Motown Sports Village Planned Near Detroit Metro Airport
articles.summary: A $3 billion mixed-use entertainment development is planned near Detroit Metro Airport, featuring an arena, water park, hotels, IMAX theater, and more. Projects of this scale represent the continued trend of large-format entertainment destinations competing for leisure spending. FEC operators in the region should take note of the competitive landscape shift this development would bring.

articles.title: Semnox Deploys Parafait Platform at Funzy Playground in Romania
articles.summary: Semnox Solutions has implemented its Parafait platform at Funzy Playground in Bacău, Romania, supporting 85 arcade games with RFID-based cashless play, time-based billing for kids' zones, and automated access control. The deployment showcases how a single integrated platform can manage diverse FEC revenue streams from arcade to soft play. This case study is a useful reference for operators evaluating cashless and access-control technology upgrades.

articles.title: Thunderzone Family Entertainment Opens New Location in Carrollton, Georgia
articles.summary: Thunderzone Family Entertainment has opened a new venue in Carrollton, Georgia, adding to the growing number of FEC openings in smaller markets. The opening reflects broader industry expansion beyond major metropolitan areas. Operators and investors targeting secondary and tertiary markets will find this development relevant as demand for local entertainment options grows.

articles.title: Spokane Valley FEC Closes Permanently After Two Years in Business
articles.summary: A family entertainment center in Spokane Valley has shut down permanently after just two years of operation, highlighting the operational challenges facing newer FEC entrants. The closure comes just ahead of the summer season, disappointing local families. This serves as a cautionary reminder for operators about the importance of sustainable business models, sufficient capitalization, and market analysis.

articles.title: K1 Speed Opens Multi-Level Indoor Go-Kart Track in Thornton, Colorado
articles.summary: K1 Speed has opened a new indoor amusement center in Thornton, Colorado featuring a multi-level go-kart track, continuing the brand's national expansion. Multi-level track designs are becoming an increasingly popular way for indoor karting operators to maximize vertical space in suburban retail locations. The opening reinforces K1 Speed's position as one of the most aggressive growth stories in the indoor karting segment.

articles.title: Cinemark's Gamescape FEC Concept Cancelled at Merriam Town Center
articles.summary: Cinemark's planned Gamescape entertainment concept has been killed at Merriam Town Center in Johnson County, Kansas, marking another setback for the theater chain's FEC diversification strategy. The cancellation raises questions about the viability of cinema-adjacent entertainment concepts in the current market. FEC operators competing in markets where Gamescape was planned may benefit from reduced competition.

articles.title: Intercard Showcases Award-Winning iQ QR Code Reader at IAAPA Expo Asia 2026
articles.summary: Intercard will exhibit its iQ cashless reader — which now supports QR code scanning in addition to RFID — at IAAPA Expo Asia in Hong Kong from June 10–12. The QR code capability expands payment flexibility for arcade and FEC operators in markets where QR-based payments dominate. FEC operators sourcing cashless technology or attending the expo should add Intercard's booth to their agenda.

articles.title: Supercharged Indoor Entertainment Concept Eyeing Polaris District Alongside Topgolf and Smash Park
articles.summary: Supercharged, an indoor entertainment concept, is being considered for the Polaris entertainment district in Ohio, which already hosts Topgolf and Smash Park. The clustering of competitive socializing and FEC concepts in a single destination area reflects a growing entertainment district model that drives collective foot traffic. Operators should study this co-location trend as it reshapes where consumers choose to spend leisure time.

articles.title: Indoor Entertainment Center Takes Lease in Former Sears Space at Sunrise Mall
articles.summary: An indoor entertainment center has signed a lease to occupy part of the former Sears anchor space at Sunrise Mall, continuing the national trend of FECs backfilling vacated department store real estate. Large-format former anchor spaces offer attractive square footage and visibility for FEC operators at often favorable lease rates. This deal is a useful data point for operators and real estate investors evaluating similar opportunities.

articles.title: OKANA Resort Fully Opens for Its First Summer Season in Oklahoma City
articles.summary: OKANA Resort in Oklahoma City is now fully open for its inaugural summer season, offering a mix of water park, hotel, and entertainment amenities. The resort represents the type of integrated entertainment destination increasingly competing for the same family leisure dollars as standalone FECs. Operators in the Oklahoma City region should monitor attendance trends and guest feedback as this new competitor hits full stride.

articles.title: Dellshire Resort Opens in Wisconsin Dells with Fire-Breathing Dragon Centerpiece
articles.summary: Dellshire Resort has opened in Wisconsin Dells, anchored by a dramatic large-scale fire-breathing dragon attraction designed to create a memorable guest centerpiece. The opening adds another entertainment option to one of the Midwest's most competitive family destination markets. The dragon feature illustrates how new venues are investing in signature spectacle elements to drive social sharing and repeat visitation.

articles.title: Mattel Wonder Indoor Waterpark Confirmed for North Carolina Resort Development
articles.summary: Mattel has confirmed a fourth US location for its Mattel Wonder Indoor Waterpark concept, to be part of the planned Weldon Mills Resort in Roanoke Rapids, North Carolina, with an expected 2029 opening. The branded indoor waterpark pipeline now includes five US locations, reflecting the strength of toy and media IP partnerships in driving new FEC-adjacent development. Operators in the indoor waterpark space should track this IP-branded competition as it scales.

articles.title: Universal Kids Resort Sets July 1 Opening Date in Frisco, Texas
articles.summary: Universal Kids Resort, the first Universal Destinations & Experiences park designed exclusively for families with young children, will open July 1, 2026 in Frisco, Texas. The 20-acre park features seven themed lands based on DreamWorks, Nickelodeon, and Illumination IP, directly targeting the same young family demographic that drives FEC revenue. Operators in the Dallas-Fort Worth region and across the family entertainment sector should factor this high-profile competitor into their summer strategies.

articles.title: ROLLER Launches Embedded Financing Product Powered by Adyen
articles.summary: ROLLER has officially launched its Capital financing solution in partnership with Adyen, enabling venue operators to access fast, embedded funding directly within the ROLLER platform. The product is designed to help entertainment venues manage cash flow and invest in growth without leaving their core operations software. FEC operators already using ROLLER should explore this feature as a potential alternative to traditional business lending.

articles.title: Play Playground Nashville to Host Rooftop Fourth of July Fireworks Party
articles.summary: Play Playground Nashville, an adult-oriented social gaming venue near Broadway, is hosting a Fourth of July rooftop fireworks viewing event featuring its 20-plus oversized games. The event highlights how FECs and entertainment venues can capitalize on holiday programming to drive traffic during typically competitive summer nights. Operators looking for seasonal event ideas should take note of this activation strategy.

articles.title: Kids Bowl Free Program Returns at Kentucky and Southern Indiana Lanes This Summer
articles.summary: Bowling centers across Kentucky and Southern Indiana are again participating in the Kids Bowl Free summer program, offering complimentary games for children to drive foot traffic during the summer months. The program is a well-established demand driver for bowling FECs looking to attract families and upsell food, beverages, and shoe rentals. Operators not yet enrolled in similar promotional programs should evaluate the ROI of participation.

articles.title: Fun Spot America Celebrates 28th Birthday with $28 Single-Day Pass Promotion
articles.summary: Fun Spot America Theme Parks is running a birthday promotional offer of $28 single-day passes — a 59% discount — with no blackout dates to celebrate its 28th anniversary. Milestone anniversary promotions like this are a proven tactic for generating buzz and driving incremental visits during the competitive summer season. FEC operators can use this as a case study for structuring their own anniversary or seasonal discount campaigns.

articles.title: Six Flags Great America Revives Racing Mode on American Eagle Coaster for 50th Anniversary
articles.summary: Six Flags Great America is restoring dual-track racing operation on its iconic American Eagle wooden coaster for the first time in nearly a decade as part of its 50th anniversary celebration. Reactivating dormant ride features at low capital cost is an effective strategy for generating renewed guest interest and media attention. This approach offers a useful lesson for FEC operators looking to refresh their offerings without major investment.

articles.title: Timezone Opens Bowling and Entertainment Complex at Festival Mall in the Philippines
articles.summary: Timezone has opened an expanded new venue at Festival Mall in the Philippines, featuring bowling alongside its traditional arcade and amusement offerings. The opening reflects Timezone's continued push to evolve from pure arcade operator to full-service FEC with diversified revenue streams. International expansion moves by major FEC brands like Timezone offer useful benchmarks for operators assessing multi-attraction format strategies.

articles.title: AAA Expo 2026 in Guangzhou Showcases Latest Amusement and Attraction Innovations
articles.summary: The Asia Amusement & Attraction Expo 2026 in Guangzhou drew exhibitors and buyers from across China and the world, featuring go-karts, interactive rides, animatronic attractions, and large-scale inflatables across a dozen exhibition halls. The show highlights the scale of Asian manufacturing and innovation that supplies the global FEC and attractions industry. Operators and procurement teams sourcing new attractions should track AAA as a key showcase for emerging product lines.

articles.title: Jenkinson's Boardwalk Nears Opening of New Patriot's Run Steel Coaster
articles.summary: Patriot's Run, a new steel coaster at Jenkinson's Boardwalk in Point Pleasant Beach, New Jersey, is completing test runs and is close to welcoming its first guests. New coaster installations at regional parks and boardwalks remain one of the most effective tools for driving media coverage and incremental visits. The addition reinforces the importance of capital investment in signature attractions for destination-oriented FEC operators.

articles.title: Moment Factory and Mitsui Fudosan Partner on Touring Immersive Pokémon Experience
articles.summary: Moment Factory has announced a multi-year strategic partnership with Mitsui Fudosan to produce a new immersive Pokémon experience debuting in Europe in Spring 2027 before touring globally. The project will use interactive technology to allow guests to step into the role of a Pokémon Trainer in a fully immersive environment. FEC operators and venue owners interested in hosting touring IP-based immersive experiences should monitor this project as a potential booking opportunity.

articles.title: Gateway Ticketing Systems Deploys Galaxy Platform Campus-Wide at John Ball Zoo
articles.summary: Gateway Ticketing Systems has gone live with its Galaxy platform at John Ball Zoo in Grand Rapids, Michigan, integrating ticketing, retail, food and beverage, and revenue management into a single system for a venue attracting 700,000-plus annual visitors. The unified platform approach reduces data silos and creates a smoother guest journey from entry to departure. FEC operators managing multiple revenue streams will find this case study relevant when evaluating their own ticketing and POS infrastructure.

articles.title: London-Based Interactive Entertainment Concept Bringing Foosball, Virtual Soccer, and Cocktails to Paris
articles.summary: A London-originating indoor entertainment concept combining foosball, virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food is expanding to Paris, representing the ongoing internationalization of competitive socializing formats. The concept follows in the footsteps of venues like Bounce and Swingers that have successfully crossed markets with gamified socializing experiences. FEC operators watching the competitive socializing segment will want to track this European expansion.

articles.title: Hasbro and Bucket Listers Launch Candy Land Café Pop-Up Experience in San Diego
articles.summary: Hasbro and Bucket Listers are opening a limited-time Candy Land Café in San Diego on July 9, featuring immersive themed environments, interactive activities, and a food and beverage menu inspired by the classic board game. The pop-up model combining IP, immersive design, and dining is a growing format that FEC operators can study for seasonal programming inspiration. Board game and toy IP activations represent an accessible partnership pathway for operators looking to create buzz-worthy limited-run events.

articles.title: Margaritaville Resort Biloxi Adopts Active Shooter Detection Technology
articles.summary: Margaritaville Resort Biloxi is implementing the Assisted Response active shooter detection platform, reflecting a growing awareness among entertainment venue operators of the need for advanced safety and security systems. As high-traffic family venues, FECs face similar security considerations and should evaluate comparable threat-detection technologies. This adoption signals a broader trend of entertainment destinations investing proactively in guest safety infrastructure.
